meaning: n.人工孵化的鱼苗或小鸟
examples: Another thing would be, if a hatchling gets separated from the rest of its family, once the others get far enough away, its survival instinct kicks in.
Breeding season is underway for most of the world's birds, with hatchling season close behind.
Scientists have found a 67 million-year-old fossil of a snake coiled around dinosaur eggs and a hatchling.
With ospreys incubating eggs for between 35-40 days, rangers hope to see a hatchling at the end of May.
It's not an easy business, given that the fish take a good nine years to grow from hatchling to roe-producing adult.
